name: chr4_centromere
description: Chromosome 4 centromere/pericentromere on the zebrafish GRCz11 reference. Zebrafish centromeres are built from the SAT-1 (~195 bp BRSATI) tandem repeat plus minor SAT-2 (~191 bp) and KS (KRT-like) repeats. Higher-order organisation is poorly resolved in GRCz11 (centromeric gaps remain in most chromosomes). Phillips & Reed 1996 cytogenetic data place the centromere here. chr4 carries the largest pericentric heterochromatin block in the zebrafish genome; the centromere is embedded in a >40 Mb tandem-satellite right arm dominated by BRSATI (Sa-I) 195-bp repeats and NLR gene clusters.
expected_copy_count: thousands of 195 bp BRSATI monomers per centromere; total ~5-10 Mb across all 25 centromeres
